meta.title: App Reference - Overview
meta.excerpt: Next.js 15 frontend architecture with atomic design and guard architecture

seo.metaDescription: Next.js 15 frontend architecture with atomic design and guard architecture

blocks.content: Comprehensive overview of the Next.js 15 application frontend architecture, featuring atomic design components, guard architecture for hydration protection, and 7-layer security implementation.

items: 10 Atoms: Form inputs, buttons, icons, badges, tags, spinners, tooltips, modals, dropdowns, loaders
14 Molecules: Form fields, cards, headers, footers, navigation items, lists, breadcrumbs, tabs, accordions, alerts, progress bars, chips, pagination, sidebars
10 Organisms: Complex containers, multi-step forms, data tables, search interfaces, filters, galleries, builders, carousels, feeds, widgets
Radix UI components use aria-controls IDs
Server and client generate different IDs on first render
User sees console warnings and potential UI glitches
Custom hook that tracks hydration state
Components render skeleton on server, content on client
Layer 1: IP-based rate limiting (prevent brute force)
Layer 2: CSRF token validation (prevent cross-site attacks)
Layer 3: Input sanitization with DOMPurify (prevent XSS)
Layer 4: Hydration guard protection (prevent mismatch exploits)
Layer 5: Server action validation with Zod (prevent invalid mutations)
Core Web Vitals: LCP <2.5s, FID <100ms, CLS <0.1
